summary: At the age of 16, after my father's sudden death, I immigrated to California with close family relatives. It was a new beginning for me, and I settled in Hayward, California. California became the place where I built my life, attended high school, and eventually started a family despite the challenges I faced.

I established myself in Hayward, California, after moving there as a teenager. It was the community where I attended San Leandro High School and began my welding career. Hayward was the backdrop of my youth and early adulthood, a place tied to both hardship and hope.

I attended San Leandro High School after moving to California. It was there during my last year that I joined a youth program which introduced me to welding. San Leandro High was also where I met Anna Wildman, the love of my life, and where we shared secret moments despite the town's racial tensions.

San Leandro was a Sundown town when I was growing up, which meant it was mostly white and enforced discrimination against people of color like me. Being out after dark was dangerous, especially for a Latino man seen with a white woman like Anna. This reality forced us to keep our relationship secret and live in fear of the consequences.

Anna Wildman was the young woman I fell in love with in the welding program at San Leandro High School. Despite the risks of being a biracial couple in a Sundown town, we met secretly and cherished every moment together. After years of dating and overcoming societal disapproval, we married and started a family, though tragically she died giving birth to our son.

Antonio Jr. was born to me and Anna Wildman on December 28, 1977, at 3:37 am. His birth was bittersweet because Anna died during childbirth, but he became my reason to keep living and honoring Anna's memory. I raised Antonio Jr. with the promise to never be belittled by anyone or anything, commemorating every sunset in Anna's name.
